title: orthion guatemalense
intro: Orthion guatemalense, also known as the Mexican evening primrose, is a flowering plant native to Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its sap and leaves. The health benefits of Orthion guatemalense include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and sap can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
